rubric_item_scores.notes: Deterministic artifact-contract precheck only; semantic correctness is not evaluated.

metadata_json.notes: No evidence of external numeric sourcing; all numeric values cite input/task_brief.md:4. Semantic correctness assessed by recomputing allocations and costs from provided artifacts.
No evidence of external numeric sourcing; all numeric values cite input/task_brief.md:4. Semantic correctness assessed by recomputing allocations and costs from provided artifacts.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Good submission. For stronger reproducibility include an explicit intermediate_calculations.txt or solver log file in artifacts (trace already shows the node script) and note explicitly that demand-splitting is allowed (or flag 'needs_review' if single-source-per-demand was intended).
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required artifacts present. Per-demand totals equal specified demands (D1=33,D2=51,D3=28) as shown in dispatch_schedule.csv and revenue_risk_note.md. Per-resource totals (R3=41,R1=46,R2=25) do not exceed capacities (R3<=41,R1<=46,R2<=64). Total cost recomputed from allocations (697+966+675=2338) matches reported Total=2338. Greedy cheapest-first allocation is optimal for linear per-unit costs with divisible demand; revenue_risk_note.md documents the sorting and arithmetic with citations to input/task_brief.md:4 for every numeric input.

limitations: Evaluation assumes the document-provided interpretation that demands may be split across resources (the artifact itself flags this as 'needs_review' if exclusivity is intended).
Revenue-side inputs (price series), degradation, efficiency, and cycle limits were not provided and thus not modeled - noted in artifacts as 'needs_review'.

This model-grade assesses feasibility, cost calculation correctness, and documentation completeness with respect to the packet; it does not evaluate broader operational constraints absent from the packet (ramping, temporal coupling, etc.).

rubric_item_scores.notes: Deterministic artifact-contract precheck only; semantic correctness is not evaluated.

metadata_json.notes: Model judged that the submission meets all primary checks: formatting, per-demand totals, capacity adherence, correct recomputation of total cost, and inclusion of citations to the supplied packet.

metadata_json.notes: Model judged that the submission meets all primary checks: formatting, per-demand totals, capacity adherence, correct recomputation of total cost, and inclusion of citations to the supplied packet.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a dedicated intermediate_calculations.txt or solver trace file (with the node script/log already shown in agent_trace) embedded in artifacts for maximum reproducibility. Also explicitly label each intermediate arithmetic line in revenue_risk_note.md with its packet citation (e.g., repeat input/task_brief.md:4 next to each summed term) and state explicitly that splitting of demands is allowed unless a one-resource-per-demand constraint is intended.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required artifacts present. Allocations meet each demand exactly (D1=33, D2=51, D3=28) and per-resource totals respect capacities (R3=41/41, R1=46/46, R2=25/64). The schedule follows cheapest-first allocation by unit cost (R3=17, R1=21, R2=27), producing total cost 2338, matching the provided arithmetic. Given linear unit costs and no exclusivity constraint, the cheapest-first allocation is optimal.
